headline: Tesla’s Earnings Are Coming. 3 Things to Watch For Before the Drama Begins.
summary: Tesla is set to report second-quarter earnings after Wednesday's market close.  For the period, Wall Street is looking for earnings per share of 55 cents and an operating profit of $1.7 billion from sales of $27.3 billion, according to FactSet.  Higher oil prices, Tesla incentives, and EV retrenchment from traditional car makers helped boost Tesla’s numbers.

content.title: Tesla’s Earnings Are Coming. 3 Things to Watch For Before the Drama Begins.
content.summary: Tesla is set to report second-quarter earnings after Wednesday's market close.  For the period, Wall Street is looking for earnings per share of 55 cents and an operating profit of $1.7 billion from sales of $27.3 billion, according to FactSet.  Higher oil prices, Tesla incentives, and EV retrenchment from traditional car makers helped boost Tesla’s numbers.

headline: Adam Smith’s ‘Wealth of Nations’ Helped Create the World’s Wealthiest Nation
summary: “Every man, as long as he does not violate the laws of justice, is left perfectly free to pursue his own interest his own way, and to bring both his industry and his capital into competition with those of any other man, or order of men,” Adam Smith wrote in , published March 9, 1776.  Thomas Jefferson didn’t get a chance to read Wealth of Nations before starting on the Declaration of Independence two months later.  Jefferson’s assault was political, arguing that a free people had the right to reject a tyrannical king.

content.title: Adam Smith’s ‘Wealth of Nations’ Helped Create the World’s Wealthiest Nation
content.summary: “Every man, as long as he does not violate the laws of justice, is left perfectly free to pursue his own interest his own way, and to bring both his industry and his capital into competition with those of any other man, or order of men,” Adam Smith wrote in , published March 9, 1776.  Thomas Jefferson didn’t get a chance to read Wealth of Nations before starting on the Declaration of Independence two months later.  Jefferson’s assault was political, arguing that a free people had the right to reject a tyrannical king.

headline: AI isn't 'the whole game' for Alphabet: Strategist previews Q2 earnings
summary: US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) are sliding in Wednesday's pre-market trading as investors eagerly await second quarter earnings from Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) and Tesla (TSLA) after today's closing bell, officially kicking off earnings season for the Magnificent Seven. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Pras Subramanian and Fundstrat economic strategist Hardika Singh to discuss the outlook around this earnings season and which segments of Alphabet's business that investors will be watching more closely.

content.title: AI isn't 'the whole game' for Alphabet: Strategist previews Q2 earnings
content.description:
US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) are sliding in Wednesday's pre-market trading as investors eagerly await second quarter earnings from Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) and Tesla (TSLA) after today's closing bell, officially kicking off earnings season for the Magnificent Seven.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Pras Subramanian and Fundstrat economic strategist Hardika Singh to discuss the outlook around this earnings season and which segments of Alphabet's business that investors will be watching more closely.
content.summary: US stock futures (ES=F, NQ=F, YM=F) are sliding in Wednesday's pre-market trading as investors eagerly await second quarter earnings from Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL) and Tesla (TSLA) after today's closing bell, officially kicking off earnings season for the Magnificent Seven. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Senior Reporter Pras Subramanian and Fundstrat economic strategist Hardika Singh to discuss the outlook around this earnings season and which segments of Alphabet's business that investors will be watching more closely.

headline: Three Things To Watch Beyond Earnings
summary: Investors should be on the lookout for updates on robo-taxis, robots, and Full Self Driving (FSD).  "Key to the earnings call is how fast Elon promises to scale up unsupervised autonomy without safety monitors," says Future Fund Active ETF cofounder Gary Black.  Any concrete guidance on the number of cars operating fully on their own could help Tesla shares.

headline: Tech Earnings Will Put a Fragile Stock Market to the Test
summary: The stock market could face its sternest test since the Iran war recovery this week, as a key component of the tech-led rally reports second-quarter earnings.  Google parent  Alphabet  the world’s third-largest stock, will update investors on its second quarter activity after the close of trading on Wednesday, likely a few minutes after Elon Musk’s  Tesla  posts results for the three months ending in June.  The reports will kick off a long stretch of big-ticket earnings releases, including  Microsoft Meta Platforms Amazon  and  Apple  next week, as investors face the market’s weeks-long stall.

headline: Tesla Stock Rises In Early Trading
summary: Tesla stock was up 18 cents in early trading at $379.11.  Coming into Wednesday trading, Tesla stock was down about 16% year-to-date and down about 2% since Tesla reported first-quarter numbers on April 22.  Tesla reported 41 cents per share in first-quarter earnings in April.

headline: Tesla Stock Is Down In Afternoon Trading
summary: Investors are waiting. Tesla shares are down 0.5% in midday trading at $377.20, ahead of the company's second-quarter earnings report on Wednesday evening. That's a small move for Tesla stock. The average daily move, up or down, over the past two years is almost 3%.

headline: Iran Didn't Give Tesla Much of a Boost in Q2
summary: Tesla sold 124,800 vehicles in the U.S. in the second quarter, according to Cox Automotive.  It's the third consecutive quarter that Tesla has captured more than half of the market.  EV sales grew 14% in the second quarter compared with the first, possibly getting a boost from higher oil prices during fighting in Iran, which made cars that run on electricity look a little more attractive.

content.title: Iran Didn't Give Tesla Much of a Boost in Q2
content.summary: Tesla sold 124,800 vehicles in the U.S. in the second quarter, according to Cox Automotive.  It's the third consecutive quarter that Tesla has captured more than half of the market. headline: Momentum Leading a Majority of Stocks Higher
summary: Momentum ETFs were living up to their names in early afternoon trading. The Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF, the Invesco Dorsey Wright Momentum ETF, and the iShares MSCI USA Momentum Factor ETF were gaining steam, while smaller stocks continued to lag.
